content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light and a deliberate cognitive act of reviewing past experiences, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to leverage this dual nature: inconsistent reasoning outputs (the "reflected light" of the LLM’s different thinking perspectives) act as the external mirror for the model to review its own problem-solving, rather than only relying on internal memory tracing of past outputs.
